name: Supabase
description: An open-source Firebase alternative built on PostgreSQL — get a full backend in minutes with a managed database, auto-generated REST & GraphQL APIs, authentication, realtime subscriptions and a beautiful Studio dashboard.

aws.service: AWS Amplify + Amazon RDS + Amazon Cognito
aws.parity: Supabase wraps standard PostgreSQL — the same schema, queries and RLS policies run on RDS; Auth maps to Cognito and Storage maps to S3.
aws.deploy: Migrate your PostgreSQL schema to RDS, replace Supabase Auth with Cognito user pools, swap Supabase Storage for S3 presigned URLs, and update client SDKs to point at the new endpoints.

variables.label: Studio Port
variables.description: Port on your host machine to access the Supabase Studio dashboard.

variables.label: PostgreSQL Port
variables.description: Port on your host machine to connect directly to the PostgreSQL database.

variables.label: Database Password
variables.description: Password for the PostgreSQL superuser account.

variables.label: JWT Secret
variables.description: Secret used to sign and verify JSON Web Tokens. Must be at least 32 characters.

variables.label: Anon Key
variables.description: Public anonymous JWT key used in client-side SDK calls. Replace with a key signed by your JWT_SECRET for production.

variables.label: Service Role Key
variables.description: Private service role JWT key that bypasses RLS. Keep this secret and use only in server-side code.
